summary: Backend regression for Drift complete. 32/32 fast tests pass (auth, feed, trips, save/steal idempotency, planner score, invites, no-booking guards). AI Planner & Remix endpoints WORK correctly when hit on localhost (verified: structured JSON with destination/trip_score/days[0].morning|afternoon|evening/budget_breakdown of 5 — generation took ~124s) but return 502 over the public preview URL because Claude Sonnet 4.5 generation takes 60-130s and the Kubernetes ingress times out at ~120s. Demo user demo@drift.app / DriftDemo123! seeded correctly. Bcrypt warning (passlib reading bcrypt.__about__) is logged but non-blocking (hashing works).

critical.issue: Endpoint returns valid itinerary when called directly (localhost:8001) in ~124s, but the public preview ingress times out at ~120s causing a 502 for end users. Reproducible: ~2-minute Claude generation > ingress read timeout. Mitigation options: (a) reduce Claude prompt scope / cap days*activities to keep generation under 90s, (b) stream/return early ack and poll, (c) use a background job + WebSocket/SSE, or (d) raise ingress timeout. Same issue affects POST /api/trips/{id}/remix.

Bug: new_doc.pop('_id', None) happens BEFORE await db.trips.insert_one(new_doc). Motor mutates new_doc and re-adds the ObjectId after insert, so the returned dict contains _id and FastAPI's jsonable_encoder fails with: ValueError: [TypeError("'ObjectId' object is not iterable"), TypeError('vars() argument must have __dict__ attribute')]. Observed in /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log. FIX: move new_doc.pop('_id', None) to AFTER insert_one (same pattern used in save/steal/create_trip endpoints).

minor.issue: passlib bcrypt version-detection warning at startup (AttributeError: module 'bcrypt' has no attribute '__about__'). Hashing still works. Consider pinning bcrypt<4 or upgrading passlib to a build that supports new bcrypt API.
Accepts any string for 'email' without EmailStr validation; also has no real send/email delivery integration (only increments invites_used). Document as MVP stub.
Not idempotent (every call creates a new stolen trip and increments stats.stolen_count). Matches PRD if intentional, but worth confirming vs save which IS idempotent.

action_items: Address AI Planner ingress timeout: shorten Claude generation latency to <90s (tighter prompt, fewer activities per slot, or cap days to <=5 in single call) OR switch to async job + polling so the HTTP request returns in <30s. This currently makes /api/planner/generate and /api/trips/{id}/remix unusable via the public URL.
Optionally pin bcrypt to <4 in requirements.txt to silence passlib warning.

Frontend env var key is EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL in /app/frontend/.env (system prompt references EXPO_BACKEND_URL) — verify the frontend actually reads process.env.EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL or Constants.expoConfig?.extra?.EXPO_BACKEND_URL consistently.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is a single 767-line file. Acceptable for MVP, but consider splitting into routers/auth.py, routers/feed.py, routers/trips.py, routers/planner.py, services/llm.py for maintainability before scaling.
Seed function is correctly idempotent (uses $setOnInsert and checks existing user/trip count).
MongoDB _id is correctly excluded everywhere via projection {'_id': 0}.
JWT_SECRET in .env is a placeholder ('drift-supersecret-jwt-change-me-in-prod') — must be rotated for production.

OAuth2PasswordBearer is configured with tokenUrl='/api/auth/login' but the login endpoint expects JSON body (LoginBody) not OAuth2 form data — works fine for our Bearer flow but Swagger 'Authorize' won't function as a form login. Cosmetic.
POST /api/invites/send body is typed as Dict[str, str] instead of a Pydantic model — minor input-validation gap.
No rate limiting on /api/planner/generate. Claude calls are expensive; consider per-user quota.

success_rate.backend: 94% (32/34 — 2 AI endpoints fail only via public URL due to ingress timeout; both verified working on localhost)

seed_data_creation: Used auto-seeded demo user (demo@drift.app) and seeded feed/trips. Tests register transient TEST_<uuid>@drift.app users (not cleaned up — acceptable for MVP).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Public URL: https://ai-travel-remix.preview.emergentagent.com. Demo creds demo@drift.app / DriftDemo123!. Fast suite runs in ~12s. For AI tests (planner/generate, trips/{id}/remix), the public URL ingress times out at ~120s; test against http://localhost:8001 to validate correctness, but report the public-URL 502 as the user-facing blocker. Save IS idempotent; steal is NOT idempotent (by design). Onboarding writes preferences and sets onboarded=true. Invites endpoint returns total=5 with remaining=total-used.
rca_of_issue: AI planner 502 root cause: Claude Sonnet 4.5 itinerary generation takes ~120-130s. The Kubernetes ingress in front of the preview URL has a ~120s read timeout, so the proxy returns 502 before Claude finishes. Direct call to http://localhost:8001/api/planner/generate succeeds with a fully populated itinerary (destination, country, summary, trip_score=94, best_time, total_estimated_cost_usd, budget_breakdown of 5 categories, days array with morning/afternoon/evening/transport/hidden_gem/weather_tip/alternative, packing_tips, local_phrases). Mitigation: (1) Tighten the ITINERARY_SYSTEM_PROMPT or cap activities-per-slot to reduce generation time below 90s, (2) switch to fire-and-poll: POST returns a job_id, GET /api/planner/jobs/{id} polls until ready, (3) request a higher ingress timeout.
